title: Acianthera sulphurea
taxonomy: Acianthera sulphurea is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a member of the genus Acianthera. The genus Acianthera is a group of small to medium-sized orchids, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Within the Orchidaceae family, Acianthera sulphurea is further classified as part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The exact classification of Acianthera sulphurea is a result of ongoing research and revisions in the field of orchid taxonomy.
uses: Acianthera sulphurea is a species of orchid, but there is limited information available about its medicinal uses.
